summary: workflow-cookbook readiness/downstream automation changes and five-tool remediation requirements audit.

requirements.original_text: Release readiness, security posture diffing, Birdseye remediation, CI phase diagnosis, adaptive improvement utilities, schema/sample/docs matrix, branch protection weekly artifacts, and downstream onboarding must be implemented and traceable to acceptance evidence.

evidence.summary: Task Seed lists the feature scope, requirements, affected paths, and acceptance link.
Acceptance record marks the automation criteria and evidence complete.

requirements.suggested_action: Maintain generated evidence in CI artifacts for downstream reuse.

requirements.original_text: Five-tool validation blockers must be resolved or explicitly reflected into the release Gate verdict before approval.

evidence.summary: Runbook defines RanD -> Code-to-gate -> HATE -> manual-bb -> QEG propagation rules.
Code-to-gate readiness passed with no critical/high blockers.
HATE real-repo evidence passed with 573 executed records and no held repos.

requirements.suggested_action: Keep QEG fixture and record with the same release evidence pack.

requirements.original_text: Console script smoke tests must work in isolated HATE real-repo environments where pip is not installed in the active Python interpreter.

evidence.summary: Editable install helper falls back to uv pip install --python when pip is unavailable.
Full pytest suite passed with 573 tests.

requirements.suggested_action: Retain python -m pytest style in HATE rosters for isolated execution.

gate_summary.overall_reason: All audited requirements are source-backed, testable, implemented, and covered by current tool evidence.

assumptions: Kano classification is used as a RanD-style audit hypothesis, not as a formal user survey.
Local C:/tmp tool outputs are the current validation evidence for this workstation run.
